LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION TO INCLUDE THE CCP SECTION 998 ALLEGATION FROM THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-29-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT GALLAGHER, ART
OPPOSITION TO SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIEK COMPLAINT [CCP 425.16]; DECLARATION OF TERENCE A. REDMOND IN OPPOS FILED BY PLAINTIFF WESLEY, KENNETH M.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. THE COMPLAINT ARISES FROM DEFENDANT'S CONSTITUTIONALLY-PROTECTED ACTIVITY. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF ESTABLISHES A PROBABILITY THAT HE WILL PREVAIL IN HIS MALICIOUS PROSECUTION CLAIMS. THE CONTRA COSTA COUNTY LAW SUIT WAS FILED BY DEFENDANT BERKO ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT GALLAGHER. DEFENDANT ONLY PREVAILED ON THE ASSAULT AND BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION AND DID NOT PREVAIL ON THE REMAINING CLAIMS WHICH ARE SEVERABLE, AND FORM THE BASIS OF THIS MALICIOUS PROSECUTION CASE. (SEE PARAMOUNT GENERAL HOSPITAL CO. VS. JAY (1989) 213 CAL.APP. 3D 360). THE JUDGMENT IN THE CONTRA COSTA COUNTY CASE ON THE DECLARATORY RELIEF ACTION WAS AGAINST GALLAGHER, THUS THERE IS PROBABLE CAUSE THAT THE PROCEEDING WAS INSTITUTED PRIMARILY FOR AN IMPROPER PURPOSE. MALICE MAY BE ESTABLISHED BY THE REJECTION OF THE CCP SECTION 998 OFFER. FURTHERMORE, THE LACK OF WRITTEN EVIDENCE OF GALLAGHER'S ALLEGED OWNERSHIP IN THE PROPERTY, IN LIGHT OF THE CLEAR LEGAL REQUIREMENT THAT SUCH WRITING BE PRODUCED, SUGGESTS AN INFERENCE OF MALICIOUS INTENT. PLAINTIFF WAS DAMAGED BY HAVING TO DEFEND HIMSELF IN THE FORMER LAWSUIT AND BY HIS INABILITY TO SELL THE HOUSE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Aug-03-2009 Continued To Oct-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. The complaint arises from defendant's constitutionally-protected activity. However, plaintiff establishes a probability that he will prevail in his malicious prosecution claims. The Contra Costa County law suit was filed by defendant Berko on behalf of defendant Gallagher. Defendant only prevailed on the assault and battery cause of action and did not prevail on the remaining claims which are severable, and form the basis of this malicious prosecution case. (See Paramount General Hospital Co. vs. Jay (1989) 213 Cal.App. 3d 360). The judgment in the Contra Costa County case on the declaratory relief action was against Gallagher, thus there is probable cause that the proceeding was instituted primarily for an improper purpose. Malice may be established by the rejection of the CCP section 998 offer. Furthermore, the lack of written evidence of Gallagher's alleged ownership in the property, in light of the clear legal requirement that such writing be produced, suggests an inference of malicious intent. Plaintiff was damaged by having to defend himself in the former lawsuit and by his inability to sell the house. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Second Cause Of Action For Malicious Prosecution
Off Calendar Apr-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as to Second Cause of Action for Malicious Prosecution. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is Granted with ten days leave to amend the first cause of action to include the CCP section 998 allegation from the second cause of action. (302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
